% Reference LaTeX document for multi-script testing (XeLaTeX)
% NOTE: This template demonstrates multi-script rendering with available system fonts.
% For full script coverage, install: sudo apt install fonts-noto fonts-noto-cjk
\documentclass{article}
\usepackage{fontspec}
\usepackage{polyglossia}

% Use system-installed Noto fonts
\setmainfont{Noto Sans}
\newfontfamily\cjkfont{Noto Sans CJK SC}

% Note: Some Noto script fonts may not be available in standard packages.
% The following scripts use the main font as fallback:
% - Arabic, Hebrew, Devanagari, Tamil, Tibetan, Ethiopic
% To get full coverage, download fonts from tests/fixtures/scripts/fonts/
% and install them system-wide: sudo cp fonts/*.ttf fonts/*.otf /usr/local/share/fonts/
% Then run: sudo fc-cache -fv

\begin{document}
\section*{Multi-Script Reference}

\subsection*{Simple LTR}
Latin: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.\\
Greek: Καλημέρα κόσμε\\
Cyrillic: Привет мир\\
Georgian: გამარჯობა მსოფლიო\\

\subsection*{Complex Indic}
Devanagari: क्योंकि क्षत्रिय (requires Noto Sans Devanagari)\\
Tamil: வணக்கம் உலகம் (requires Noto Sans Tamil)\\

\subsection*{RTL}
Arabic: مرحبا بالعالم (requires Noto Sans Arabic)\\
Hebrew: שלום עולם (requires Noto Sans Hebrew)\\

\subsection*{CJK}
Chinese: {\cjkfont 你好，世界}\\
Japanese: {\cjkfont こんにちは世界}\\
Korean: {\cjkfont 안녕하세요 세계}\\

\subsection*{Tibetan}
བོད་ཀྱི་སྐད་ (requires Noto Sans Tibetan)

\subsection*{African}
Ethiopic: ሰላም ዓለም (requires Noto Sans Ethiopic)

\end{document}
